mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate pinned near the zero lower bound reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, creating a persistent tailwind for equity valuations. As long as FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, forward S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days are biased positive relative to historical baseline.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS<0.5% periods are not statistically greater than unconditional mean (t-test p>0.05), or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is elevated above 5% but declines by more than 0.2 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window, this signals an active labor market recovery that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.2pp over trailing 3 months while level remains above 5%
failure_condition: Triggered instances show 60-day forward returns below or equal to unconditional baseline in more than 50% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that is not detectable from either series alone, jointly predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal underperforms either single-series signal in out-of-sample Rank IC comparison, or combined trigger produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) signals improving manufacturing output and corporate profitability outlook, producing a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the next 5 trading days as markets price in the surprise.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns conditional on positive INDPRO momentum are not significantly different from unconditional returns (p>0.05) across a 2-year rolling test window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth accelerates above 3% while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero, negative real interest rates initially support risk assets, but as inflation overshoot builds expectations of future tightening, S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter turn negative once the CPI threshold is crossed.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%
regime_hint: zero rate lower bound with rising inflation risk
failure_condition: Triggered instances yield positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC magnitude stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS increasing) and unemployment rate decline (UNRATE falling) jointly confirm genuine labor market tightening rather than measurement noise, and this combined confirmation predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month more reliably than either series alone.
failure_condition: Joint-signal Rank IC does not exceed either single-series Rank IC by a statistically meaningful margin (bootstrap CI overlap), or combined Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts (HOUST) of more than 5% over a trailing 3-month window signals renewed residential investment demand that propagates through construction, durable goods, and consumer confidence channels, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 5% over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: Triggered instances show 60-day forward returns not significantly above baseline (p>0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions where real GDP (GDPC1) growth is already elevated, further acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals capacity overheating and margin compression risk rather than fresh demand, reversing the typical positive INDPRO-equity relationship into a negative one over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 3% for two consecutive quarters while INDPRO accelerates
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive or flat 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC fails to exceed 0.03 in magnitude over any 3-year rolling window.
